name: Day 19: a visitor! and mile 275!
description:
Had a gorgeous day of mostly downhill into the little trail town of Hot Springs, NC - the trail runs right down their Main Street! Earned a big burger, fried pickles, hummus wrap, and some beers at Spring Creek Tavern and watched my worlds collide as I introduced Noah to my little trail family. 

End mile: 275.4

name: Day 18: a shower, a dog, an alien building, and a visit from my favorite person!
description:
Earned my visit from Noah Cracknell with a monster day of climbing - almost 6,000 feet of elevation gain! Walked under I-40, took an outdoor shower and mini-resupply at Standing Bear Farm, had lunch at the alien headquarters, then finished off the day introducing Noah to my backcountry dinner concoctions! 

End mile: 257.1

name: Day 17: see ya Smokies!
description:
Smokies, you were something. You were freezing cold, hot and sunny, quiet sometimes, a party other times, gave us bears and deer and rattlesnakes, and a whole lot of climbing, but incredible views. Happy to be through with ya 

End mile: 240.4

name: Day 16: frolicking and playing
description:
Some days you do 10 miles by 10am, some days you do a single mile and then play on some rocks. Have a little fun with it! Also notable: new ingredient hit the classic PB&J, I had several courses for dinner at 3:30pm (including a dessert burrito), and the low was in the 20s overnight!!

End mile: 223.7

name: Day 15: all roads lead to Cici’s pizza / a town full of trail magic
description:
Hard to do this perfect day justice. Woke up at 4:40am to hike Kuwohi, the highest point on the Appalachian Trail, at sunrise. Hiked a few more hours (crossing the 200 mile mark!) and then hitchhiked into Gatlinburg for an all you can eat pizza and salad buffet, and our hitch went in ahead of us and paid for our meals! The laundromat pulled out loaner clothes and free detergent for us, the outfitter gave us a room to hang out and store our backpacks during the day, and then we got the world’s coolest hitchhike back into the Smokies right when we were starting to lose hope. The trail really does provide, and the people here are SO incredibly generous! 

End mile: 211.1

name: Day 14: back and forth between TN/NC
description:
Honestly the most normal day so far on trail until we arrived at a shelter to a man sharpening the biggest machete I’ve ever seen! Luckily he used it to build a fire, which was a godsend for the frigid night we got

End mile: 197.5

name: Day 13: God’s country? No. Bear country.
description:
Head on a swivel for bears in the Smokies but saw none until our extremely persistent friend at Russell Fields shelter. I was somehow nominated to be on bear duty so I stayed strapped with rocks in one hand and bear spray in the other! Kept the bear away until nighttime and then woke up to a chomp mark in my shoe. Thank you Mr. Bear 

End mile: 181.1

name: Day 12: to the edge of the smokies!
description:
Pretty uneventful day, but walked 18 miles, shuttled into Fontana Village for a burger, and am camping at the edge of the Smokies! 

End mile: 166.2

name: Day 11: just happy to be here!
description:
Don’t be fooled by the short mileage, this was so hard! Started my day with a diabolical 4000 foot climb out of the NOC with my heaviest pack yet - I’m carrying 5 days of food to make it through the first half of the smokies, and my pack is 34 pounds! Was a beautiful day though and caught Sam a few times at viewpoints for a chat. Camping tonight and then to the Smokies tomorrow ⛰️⛰️

End mile: 147.7

name: Day 10: we’re so back
description: Woke up after the Worst Night Of All Time™ to a foggy cold day for the first 8 miles. Fog burned off and the skies cleared for the most incredible views of the trail so far, plus some trail magic from Pastor Steve and a stop for the night at the NOC!

name: Day 9: the one where everything went wrong
description: Holy hard day. Made a quick town stop into Franklin, NC for a resupply and my first vegetable of the trail in an attempt to beat scurvy. And thennn the day went so downhill after that! Torrential downpour, gunshots (?), lightning and thunder, and 12 miles to an absolutely packed campsite where every attempt to dry my clothes was unsuccessful. Also, I fell off a log mid-pee and almost fell off a cliff. Sweet!

name: Day 8: 100 miles complete!
description: Almost my first 20 miler! Easy-ish terrain until a crazy climb up to the fire tower on Albert Mountain that marks the first 100 miles of the Appalachian Trail. Hiking felt so good today and the weather was gorgeous but we’re about to pay the price in the rain tomorrow!

name: Day 7: see ya GA, hello NC!
description: Had the butterflies for company today on a long solo 18 miles! Took me on a blue blaze to the wreckage of a plane crash, and finally met back up with Charlotte and Sam at Standing Indian for an incredible sunset and a great tent city!

name: Day 6: last full day in GA!
description:
Slackpacked the 17 miles between Unicoi Gap and Dick’s Creek Gap and felt light as a feather and even got a little running in there! It’s just hard to watch the miles go by so slowly when I’m used to clicking off a mile in less than 10 minutes and now they take 20-30. Especially when I’m hiking alone! But I’m back at Stanimals for one more night and then I’ll cross over to North Carolina tomorrow!

End mile: 69.3

name: Day 5
description: 10 miles done and dusted before noon! First hostel stay tonight and I have never been more excited for a shower, a frozen pizza, and the Celtics

name: Day 4
description:
Long day! Saw the shoe tree at Neels Gap (where people who quit the trail on day 3 throw their shoes up), did a mini resupply at Mountain Crossings at Walasi-Yi, and rejoined my new friends from yesterday back at Low Gap Shelter!

End mile: 43

name: Day 3
description:
Survived a scary thunderstorm in my tent last night and was rewarded with low humidity, stunning views, and some new friends today!

End mile: 27.7

name: Day 2
description:
This is hard man

End mile: 13.6

name: Day 1
description:
The approach trail to the start of the Appalachian Trail being 8 miles is actually incredibly on brand

End mile: 1

name: Bittersweet!
description: One last Sunny Girl with the Sunny King

name: GMMMMM
description: Last Boston endorphins star jump :( 🌟🌟
